response.justification: The role centers on a C++-based internal cloud compute platform with a hard minimum of 8 years of C++, but the candidate's professional C++ exposure is limited to roughly 7 months at National Instruments plus a university thesis project. Their core strength is full-stack web work in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. While the Sociomantic/Dunnhumby microservices experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, low-latency systems) and some Kubernetes/AWS exposure are genuinely relevant to large-scale infrastructure, the dominant technical requirement of the role is essentially unmet, making this a poor fit for a C++ technical leadership position.
strengths: Large-scale, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of req/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev, plus mentoring via workshops and courses
Breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and some cloud/Kubernetes/AWS CDK
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ minimum (only ~7 months of professional C++)
No production cloud compute platform architecture at the scale described
No formal technical leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
